description_long: This object is a cylindrical metal bollard with a smooth, uniform surface and a rounded, dome-shaped top. The body is vertically elongated and appears to be constructed from a single piece of metal, likely steel, giving it a robust and industrial appearance. At the base, there is a circular, flat mounting plate with evenly spaced holes, suggesting it is designed to be bolted securely to a hard surface such as concrete or asphalt. The surface finish is matte to slightly smooth, with no visible decorative elements, reflective markings, or color variations. The bollard is utilitarian in design, intended to serve as a physical barrier to control vehicle or pedestrian access in urban environments. Its solid construction and lack of movable or interactive parts indicate it is meant to be a permanent, immovable fixture.
description: A tall, cylindrical metal bollard with a rounded dome top and a flat circular base plate for surface mounting. The surface is smooth and matte, with a robust, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full cylindrical body, rounded dome top, and the circular base plate with bolt holes clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the uniform cylindrical shape and the smooth transition from the body to the dome top, with the base plate still visible.
description_view_2: Another side view, nearly identical to view 1, emphasizing the consistent diameter and the seamless construction of the bollard.
description_view_3: Rear view mirrors the front, showing the same cylindrical body, dome top, and base plate, with no additional features.
